summerCareTips.title: Apply Sunscreen Every 2 Hours
summerCareTips.details: Use a broad-spectrum SPF 30 or higher sunscreen and reapply every 2 hours, or immediately after swimming or sweating. Don't forget your ears, the back of your neck, and the tops of your feet.

summerCareTips.title: Stay Hydrated All Day
summerCareTips.details: Drink at least 8–10 glasses of water per day during summer. Add slices of lemon, cucumber, or mint for a refreshing twist. Carry a reusable insulated bottle to keep your drink cold for hours.

summerCareTips.title: Wear UV-Protective Eyewear
summerCareTips.details: Choose sunglasses that block 99–100% of UVA and UVB radiation. Wrap-around styles offer the best protection by blocking light from all angles. Never look directly at the sun, even with sunglasses on.

summerCareTips.title: Seek Shade During Peak Hours
summerCareTips.details: UV radiation is strongest between 10 AM and 4 PM. Use a beach umbrella, find natural shade under trees, or wear a wide-brim hat to significantly reduce your UV exposure during peak hours.

summerCareTips.title: Moisturize After Sun Exposure
summerCareTips.details: After a day in the sun, apply an aloe vera gel or a hydrating after-sun lotion to soothe and restore your skin. Avoid products with alcohol as they can dry your skin out further.

summerCareTips.title: Wear Lightweight, Light-Colored Clothing
summerCareTips.details: Loose-fitting, light-colored clothes in natural fabrics like cotton or linen reflect sunlight and allow airflow. Look for garments with a UPF (Ultraviolet Protection Factor) rating for built-in sun protection.
